unmapped.human_action: Watch for language register shift in tech media: confessional ('I tried giving up my phone') maturing into resolved ('I switched'). Monitor Light Phone, Punkt, and Mudita for fundraising or retail distribution announcements. Track whether any telecom packages a feature-phone-optimized plan without data.

signals: {'source': 'Embedded (embedded.substack.com)', 'type': 'editorial_mention', 'date': '2026-06-03', 'quote': 'The writer thinks giving up smartphones is completely doable. The writer and podcaster found the secret for spending less time on your phone.', 'strength': 'weak', 'notes': 'First-person retrospective register — not aspirational but resolved. Strength is the framing shift from experiment to stable practice; weakness is single source with no transaction, search volume, or demographic corroboration. Same publication used in the 2026-06-03 issue on AI-production vocabulary (different topic).'}
original_issue.thesis: Tech media professionals are normalizing smartphone abandonment — not experimenting with it — and the onboarding infrastructure for the second wave of adopters has not been built.
